title: Step 02: Classification Performance on candidate genes and subsequent analysis
text: list genes ready, can proceed training explainability scores extraction different classifiers. , show example use ML models training evaluating final candidate genes list. First, define global variables relative paths: ML models function uses gene list expression data. can load directly obtain using functions “Step 01: Feature Selection” vignette: Performance Radar Charts: Test (left) CV (right)
code: # Parameters target_var <- "dis_condition" # Relative paths base_dir <- "test" raw_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "raw_data") feature_selection_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "feature_selection") genes <- "candidate" ml_models_dir <- paste0("test/results_", genes) ml_models_to_run_vector <- c("rf", "knn", "svm_r", "svm_p", "glm", "xgb") # Load gene list (already pre-computed) gene_list <- load_gene_list(genes_list = genes,                base_path = feature_selection_dir) # Extract train and test sets (using function) split_data <- obtain_split_data(directory_to_load = raw_dir,                                 file_name = "expression",                                 target_var = target_var,                                 directory_to_save = NULL) expression_train <- split_data$train expression_test <- split_data$test Read 172 items [2026-03-04 14:21:36] :     Class imbalance: train set  [2026-03-04 14:21:36] :     COPD: 67.111, CTRL: 32.889  [2026-03-04 14:21:36] :     Class imbalance: test set  [2026-03-04 14:21:36] :     COPD: 67.105, CTRL: 32.895 print_message("Running ML models on candidate gene list...") results <- ML_models(   data = expression_train,   target_var = target_var,   models_to_run = ml_models_to_run_vector,   directory_to_save = ml_models_dir ) print_message("Extracting model results...") models_results <- extract_models_results(   models_to_run = ml_models_to_run_vector,   results_models = results,   expression_train = expression_train,   expression_test = expression_test,   target_var = target_var ) [2026-02-20 16:12:14] :     Running ML models on candidate gene list...  ... Saving multiple .Rda objects to test/results_candidate/ML_models/rf_model.Rda ...RF done Saving multiple .Rda objects to test/results_candidate/ML_models/svm_r_model.Rda ...SVM-rad done Saving multiple .Rda objects to test/results_candidate/ML_models/svm_p_model.Rda ...SVM-poly done Saving multiple .Rda objects to test/results_candidate/ML_models/glm_model.Rda ...GLM done Saving multiple .Rda objects to test/results_candidate/ML_models/knn_model.Rda ...kNN done Saving multiple .Rda objects to test/results_candidate/ML_models/xgb_model.Rda ...XGB done [2026-02-20 19:36:38] :     Extracting model results... # results <- readRDS("/home/iria/bsc008817/COPD/EBEx/test/results_candidate/models_results.rds") # Prepare data and plot print_message("Processing results for plotting...") models_results_cv_df <- process_cross_validation_metrics(   results_models = models_results,   classifiers = ml_models_to_run_vector ) models_results_test_df <- process_test_metrics(   results_models = models_results,   classifiers = ml_models_to_run_vector ) models_results_cv_df$classifier <- rename_classifier(models_results_cv_df$classifier) models_results_cv_df$input_list <- rep("Candidate List", nrow(models_results_cv_df)) models_results_test_df$classifier <- rename_classifier(models_results_test_df$classifier) models_results_test_df$input_list <- rep("Candidate List", nrow(models_results_test_df))  data_test <- prepare_data_for_radarchart(models_results_test_df, metric, "estimate", minmax[1], minmax[2]) data_cv <- prepare_data_for_radarchart(models_results_cv_df, metric, "mean", minmax[1], minmax[2])  # Parameters minmax <- c(0.5,0.9,0.1) metric <- "normMCC"  # Plotting (output_dir = NULL for showing plots here) plot_radarchart(data_cv, metric, "cv", output_dir = NULL, minmax[1], minmax[2], minmax[3]) plot_radarchart(data_test, metric, "test", output_dir = NULL, minmax[1], minmax[2], minmax[3])

title: Step 01: Feature Selection
text: first step EBEx pipeline identify potential genes multiple sources. example show obtain Data-Driven Disease-Related genes. First, define global variables relative paths: get feature selection list, need train expression matrix target variable. Therefore, first step obtain train test sets:
code: # Parameters target_var <- "dis_condition" disease_code <- "C0024117" # Relative paths base_dir <- "../test" raw_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "raw_data") output_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "feature_selection") # Extract train and test sets split_data <- obtain_split_data(directory_to_load = raw_dir,                                 file_name = "expression",                                 target_var = target_var,                                 directory_to_save = output_dir) expression_train <- split_data$train expression_test <- split_data$test #> [2026-03-04 10:28:43] :     Class imbalance: train set  #> [2026-03-04 10:28:43] :     COPD: 67.111, CTRL: 32.889  #> [2026-03-04 10:28:43] :     Class imbalance: test set  #> [2026-03-04 10:28:43] :     COPD: 67.105, CTRL: 32.895

title: Step 01: Feature Selection
text: obtaining Data-Driven list need Differentially Expressed genes (DEA genes; saved dea.txt) mRMR genes. already computed DEA genes; saved dea.txt file one gene per line. obtaining mRMR genes, need ./mrmr binary program selected threshold (= 0.031 case).
code: # This code block requires the mRMR binary genes_mrmr <- run_feature_selection(   procedure = "data_driven",   expression_data = expression_train,   threshold_value = 0.031,   target_var = target_var,   dea_genes = NULL,   mrmr_genes = NULL,   directory_to_load = output_dir,   directory_to_save = output_dir ) print_message(genes_mrmr) #> [2026-03-04 10:30:02] :     Obtaining data driven genes  #> [2026-03-04 10:30:02] :     Obtaining mrmr genes  #> Read 76 items

title: Step 01: Feature Selection
text: obtaining Disease-Related genes, use DisGeNet database. , downloaded disease-associated genes curated resources can extract genes already associated disease code (COPD: C0024117).
code: # This code block requires having the DisGeNet database downloaded and knowing the disease code raw_dir <- file.path(raw_dir, "disgenet_tables") genes_disgenet <- run_feature_selection(   procedure = "disease_related",   target_var = target_var,   disease_code = disease_code,   expression_data = expression_train,   directory_to_load = raw_dir,   directory_to_save = output_dir ) print_message(genes_disgenet) #> [2026-03-04 10:38:53] :     Obtaining disease related curated genes  #> [2026-03-04 10:38:54] :     KLF5, CD8A, CYP1A1, CYP1A2, DSP, ELN, EPHX1, FOXO3, CXCL1, CXCL2, HDAC2, HMOX1, HTR2A, IL6, CXCL8, MMP1, MMP9, MMP14, NOS2, NOS3, SERPINA1, SFTPD, SOD3, TGFB1, TNF, TNNT2, TP53, FAM13A, RAPGEF3, MTCL1, TRPV4, EEFSEC, MIR218-2

what: An Ensemble-Based Explainable Framework for Gene Calling in Heterogeneous Diseases
title: An Ensemble-Based Explainable Framework for Gene Calling in Heterogeneous Diseases
text: EBEx R package integrates Ensemble-Based Explainable AI pipeline prioritising relevant genes complex diseases transcriptomic data, using Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) use case. hypothesise single classifier universally optimal modelling heterogeneous disease COPD. Therefore, formulated COPD molecular analysis supervised prediction task (case vs. control) implemented explainable ensemble-based ML framework identify robust biologically meaningful gene signatures.

title: An Ensemble-Based Explainable Framework for Gene Calling in Heterogeneous Diseases
text: can install development version EBEx GitHub directly R :

title: An Ensemble-Based Explainable Framework for Gene Calling in Heterogeneous Diseases
text: work presents computational pipeline based ensemble explainable AI methods identification robust COPD-associated genes. formulate COPD molecular analysis supervised prediction task (patients vs. controls). Within framework, pipeline identifies candidate markers capturing genes general effects disease status well genes associated specific patient subgroups. maximise sensitivity robustness, approach integrates predictions multiple well-established ML classifiers, including Random Forest (RF) Support Vector Machines (SVM). models trained several complementary candidate gene lists. candidate lists include: Genes strong signal--noise ratios derived univariate analyses (DEA) using multivariate strategies (mRMR) Genes previously reported literature (DisGeNET) Additional genes belonging biological pathways, allowing capture shared molecular mechanisms may contribute disease (OmniPath, GUILDify) Model explainability scores used quantify contribution gene predictive task. scenario, sample-specific absolute explainability scores : Normalised Aggregated gene Combined across modelling scenarios using maximum score aggregation strategy captures gene relevance multiple methodological perspectives. resulting relevance scores ranked define final candidate gene set, subsequently validated independent cohorts. methodological diversity embedded pipeline central performance. integrating classifiers, gene lists, explainability metrics, framework able recover COPD-relevant genes may overlooked single models narrowly defined feature sets. particularly important given molecular heterogeneity inherent COPD. Finally, predictive signals translated biological insight, enabling: Identification potential biomarkers Characterisation regulatory mechanisms Definition molecular COPD endotypes

title: Run ML Pipeline — ML_models
text: function serves wrapper run multiple machine learning models given dataset. takes training data, target variable, list models run, executes corresponding model training functions. results model, including training time, resampling results, fitted model objects, collected list returned. Optionally, trained models resampling results can saved specified directory.

title: Run ML Pipeline — ML_models
code: ML_models(   data,   target_var,   models_to_run = c("rf", "svm_r", "svm_p", "glm", "knn", "xgb"),   directory_to_save = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Run ML Pipeline — ML_models
text: data Data frame containing training data. target_var Name target variable. models_to_run Vector model names run. directory_to_save Directory save trained models results (optional).

title: Run ML Pipeline — ML_models
text: list containing results model, including training time, resampling results, fitted model objects.

title: Generate Gene-Sample Matrix — aggregate_gene_sample_matrix
text: function takes list normalized SHAP dataframes (one per model) creates wide matrix rows represent genes columns represent samples. values matrix mean normalized SHAP values gene-sample combination, averaged across models. 'absolute' parameter allows user choose whether use absolute values normalized SHAP values keep original sign, depending whether want focus magnitude importance also consider direction effect.

title: Generate Gene-Sample Matrix — aggregate_gene_sample_matrix
Generate Gene-Sample Matrix — aggregate_gene_sample_matrix
text: normalized_shap_list named list normalized SHAP dataframes. absolute Logical. TRUE, use absolute values normalized SHAP; FALSE, use original values.

title: Generate Gene-Sample Matrix — aggregate_gene_sample_matrix
text: wide matrix Gene x Sample.

title: Generate Gene-Level Aggregation — aggregate_genes
text: function takes list normalized SHAP dataframes (one per model) aggregates importance gene across models. calculates several metrics gene, including maximum, mean, median, standard deviation mean normalized SHAP values, count many models included gene. resulting summary dataframe sorted maximum aggregated importance, allowing easy identification top candidate genes.

title: Generate Gene-Level Aggregation — aggregate_genes
Generate Gene-Level Aggregation — aggregate_genes
text: normalized_shap_list named list normalized SHAP dataframes.

title: Generate Gene-Level Aggregation — aggregate_genes
text: summary dataframe metrics per gene.

what: Calculate SHAP values for all ML models — calculate_all_shap
title: Calculate SHAP values for all ML models — calculate_all_shap
text: function iterates list machine learning models, calculates SHAP values model using calculate_variable_importance_batch function, compiles results list. also ensures necessary package explaining tidymodels available handles renaming model labels better readability output.

title: Calculate SHAP values for all ML models — calculate_all_shap
code: calculate_all_shap(   ml_models_to_run,   results_models,   expression_data,   target_var,   directory_to_save = NULL )

title: Calculate SHAP values for all ML models — calculate_all_shap
text: ml_models_to_run Character vector model names. results_models List fitted models (output ML_models). expression_data Dataframe. data used training. target_var Character. target variable column name. directory_to_save Character. Optional directory path.

title: Calculate SHAP values for all ML models — calculate_all_shap
text: list containing SHAP values model time taken entire process.

title: Perform Sample Clustering — calculate_sample_clustering
text: function computes distance matrix based provided method (either "euclidean" "cosine") performs hierarchical clustering samples. cuts resulting dendrogram specified number clusters (k) returns hierarchical clustering object cluster assignments sample.

title: Perform Sample Clustering — calculate_sample_clustering
Perform Sample Clustering — calculate_sample_clustering
text: mat Matrix. Expression SHAP matrix (genes rows, samples columns). method Character. "euclidean" "cosine". k Integer. Number clusters cut.

title: Perform Sample Clustering — calculate_sample_clustering
text: list containing hierarchical clustering object cluster assignments.

title: Calculate Variable Importance for a Batch of Samples — calculate_variable_importance_batch

code: calculate_variable_importance_batch(   explainer,   train_data,   target_var,   n_samples,   directory_to_save = NULL )

text: list containing summary dataframe mean SHAP values time taken process.

what: Compute Density and Derivatives for Ranking — compute_density_analysis
title: Compute Density and Derivatives for Ranking — compute_density_analysis
text: function calculates density aggregated SHAP values computes slopes window-based differences help identify cut-(Knee/Elbow method).

title: Compute Density and Derivatives for Ranking — compute_density_analysis
Compute Density and Derivatives for Ranking — compute_density_analysis

title: Create Preprocessing Recipe — create_recipe
text: function creates preprocessing recipe machine learning models using recipes package. includes steps optional Box-Cox transformation, correlation filtering remove highly correlated predictors, downsampling address class imbalance, optional normalization predictors.

title: Create Preprocessing Recipe — create_recipe
code: create_recipe(data, target_var, transformation = NULL, normalize = FALSE)

title: Create Preprocessing Recipe — create_recipe
text: data Data frame containing training data. target_var Character string target variable column name. transformation Character string specifying type transformation apply (e.g., "boxcox"). Default NULL (transformation). normalize Logical. Whether apply normalization predictors. Default FALSE.

title: Create Preprocessing Recipe — create_recipe
text: recipes recipe object can used machine learning workflow.

title: Post-DEA Analysis and Diagnostics — dea_analysis
text: function takes fitted model object DEA analysis performs post-analysis diagnostics. generates several plots visualize results, including Venn diagram DEGs, histogram p-values, mean-difference (MD) plot. function also returns matrix indicating genes differentially expressed based specified p-value log-fold change thresholds. generated plots saved "DEA_plots" subdirectory within specified output directory.

title: Post-DEA Analysis and Diagnostics — dea_analysis
Post-DEA Analysis and Diagnostics — dea_analysis
text: fit_object MArrayLM object. Output run_dea_limma. pv Numeric. P-value threshold. lfc Numeric. Log-fold change threshold. coef Integer. Coefficient analyze. output_dir Character. Directory save plots.

title: Post-DEA Analysis and Diagnostics — dea_analysis
text: results Matrix DEGs.

title: Extract models results (cross-validation, train, test and timing) — extract_models_results

what: Enrichment Analysis of Clusters vs Phenotypes — run_cluster_enrichment
title: Enrichment Analysis of Clusters vs Phenotypes — run_cluster_enrichment
text: Performs "Cluster vs Rest" statistical tests phenotypic variable. numeric variables, tests normality applies either t-test Wilcoxon test. categorical variables, applies chi-squared test. function returns dataframe summarizing results, including adjusted p-values multiple testing. Clusters fewer 3 samples automatically skipped ensure statistical validity. results include type test used, p-values, test statistics, enriched classes categorical variables.
